summary: sea-bound-siren postinstall exfiltrates host info and runs shell commands
details: The postinstall.js script executed automatically on `npm install` collects host reconnaissance data (os.hostname(), process.cwd()) and reads files from the filesystem, then transmits this information over HTTP. The script also imports child_process and invokes curl multiple times (lines 59-61) to perform additional network operations, and uses http.request() to communicate with endpoints including 127.0.0.1. The combination of automatic install-time execution, system fingerprinting, filesystem reads, and outbound HTTP/curl activity is the canonical install-time exfiltration shape and provides no legitimate functionality for a package by this name.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
